inline_comments.message: I think this part of commit msg can be removed.
Seems a little out of place, maybe it would be better to log whole config here. Also parseCloudConfig is called for 'loadConfigurationFromFile' so it's not always consul config.

Exception can't be thrown here.

Consider inlining this method in createJschClient, unless this way is better for test purposes.

1. Consider extract to method called "getKnownHostsFilePath" or "getKnownHostsFilePathFromSysEnv"
2. String "KNOWN_HOSTS_FILE_PATH" should be const
Consider to introduce strategy or factory pattern to return StrinctHost/NoStrictHost session
I think we shouldn't allow to open connection if KNOWN_HOSTS file will not be provided. Consider to throwing exception + logging message.
useStrictHostKeyChecking ? "yes" : "no"  I think it should be extracted to method.
DONE - change log level to debug and log whole config.
I think a strategy would be an "overkill" in this simple case.
Exception removed from signature. Indeed its done for testing purpose.
As far as i remember as a result of the discussion with the architect we agreed on a fallback in case known hosts file is not mounted.
I think method "shouldStrictHostkeyChecking()" contains nested if,else,if,else and it is good point to introduce some pattern or refactor current solution.

Would like to understand why default file name is called "*_test"?
Does it mean that such config is used only during testing phase and in production such file is not used and endpoints are taken from other location? Or this file brings default configuration which can be overwritten by configuration from other place (e.g. CBS)?

This is a good question.
I analyzed the code it works like this, that the app on startup loads the configuration from the file specified in application.yaml. Then it overrides it from the configuration take from CBS.
In fact what i did here is to adjust the path because for local development the config json was not found.
However it's good observation - probably the intent was to be able to develop it locally without CBS.

I know it's a bit confusing however getProperty() is correct here.
See also class EnvironmentProcessor.
same here could be avoided with docker-compose
Add Modification copyrights here and other touched files.
Is that needed for test purposes?
I would prefer for test purposes use docker-compose and there mount volume with test config. This way we will avoid commented lines here.
Move this right after other mkdirs (line 27)
I think we should consider creating docker compose file in separate story.
I think we should consider creating docker compose file in separate story.
It's done in purpose here, i.e. after creating 'datafile' user.
move this before ENRTYPOINT since this part will be most frequently chenged in image building
Do we need to define it at Dockerfile level?

make private
I would rename to "KNOWN_HOSTS_FILE_PATH_ENV_NAME"
I think we could have here also systemEnvironment. What do you think?
Make constant from it. Name could be like: STRICT_HOST_CHECKING_FIELD_NAME
Move private with privates func in order of usage

Please add here test case where stricthostchecking is enabled. We could check session configs to verify everything worked fine. Check if thats possible.
```
sftpClientSpy.session.getConfig("StrictHostKeyChecking").equals(false);
```
Because it was places 2 times, as you can see it's present below.
I think the logic in present form is OK.

Consider moving it to default in code instead of Dockerfile. Like:
 systemEnvironment.getProperty("KNOWN_HOSTS_FILE_PATH", "home/datafile/.ssh/known_hosts")
Private. Consider using naming convention from variables above - SFTP_SECURITY_STRICT_HOST_KEY_CHECKING

I think expose can stay higher in the dockerfile.
Generally you want to keep less often changing parts in the beginning as they can be cached. When image is building when there is first step that there was any change (like changing application.yaml) any step after that is gonna be ran instead of using cache.
Reference: https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/dockerfile_best-practices/#leverage-build-cache

As far as I know it is also useful for pulling and pushing images because layers are also reused.
Kornel proposition reduces a code complexity and the code is cleaner. If it is not a problem, pls change it. Thanks.
But this name tells at least exactly what property is meant.
Why do you need curl and vim? If only for testing/local deployment you should install them manually and they shouldn't be a part of production docker.
Two methods with the same implementation
As discussed vim and curl will stay.
Do we need it? I see that jupiter-engine already contains this.

We are exposing method in order to test it. I think its worth to extract class including this and other needed methods. Could be named ```StrictHostCheckingValidator```. Than added tests will be done from its level.
Move to test file of "validator" class
It works not without this dependency
What is the purpose of this method? A Boolean is automatically unboxed to a boolean by the compiler.
What is the purpose of this method?
this.sftpConfig.strictHostKeyChecking() might be null. Null is treated as false.
why is this switched back to 8?
We will fix it in separate MR.
